incidents.vector: Hackers breached the finance ministry's computer systems and diverted outgoing payments.
incidents.impact: $2.5M stolen from the ministry; a separate $600K payment owed to the US Postal Service is now unaccounted for, raising fresh questions about Sri Lanka's financial controls.

incidents.vector: Attackers compromised a school district employee's email account and impersonated the board of education to redirect tax payments.
incidents.impact: $4.8M in local tax funds stolen; the borough is issuing short-term notes to cover the shortfall while officials try to recover the money.

incidents.victim: Manufacturing, education, healthcare and technology firms (Vect 2.0 victims)
incidents.vector: Vect 2.0 ransomware deployed against organizations affected by the TeamPCP supply-chain compromises; a flaw in its ChaCha20 implementation discards three of four nonces, permanently destroying any file over 128KB.
incidents.impact: Even victims who pay the ransom cannot recover the bulk of their data; Vect effectively functions as a wiper across Windows, Linux and ESXi. Check Point urges victims not to pay.

incidents.vector: Compromise of Vimeo's third-party analytics vendor Anodot; attackers (ShinyHunters) used stolen authentication tokens to reach customer cloud environments.
incidents.impact: Technical data, video titles, metadata and some customer email addresses accessed; ShinyHunters added Vimeo to its leak site demanding a ransom by Thursday. Video content, user logins and payment data not exposed.

incidents.vector: Theft via the same compromised Anodot business analytics provider used to reach Vimeo and others.
incidents.impact: Sensitive corporate data stolen via the third-party Anodot breach and added to ShinyHunters' leak site.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to the marine fuel procurement system at the Japanese shipping company.
incidents.impact: Compromise of operational fuel procurement systems disclosed; investigation underway to determine scope and data exposure.

incidents.vector: Mass email flooding followed by Microsoft Teams chats from accounts impersonating corporate IT support; victims were tricked into installing a fake "Mailbox Repair Utility" that deployed the SnowBelt malicious browser extension along with SnowGlaze and SnowBasin malware.
incidents.impact: Persistent backdoor access to corporate browser sessions and accounts; credential harvesting and lateral movement through SaaS applications.

incidents.victim: Retail and hospitality organizations (BlackFile / Cordial Spider)
incidents.vector: Voice-phishing impersonating IT help desks, fake single-sign-on portals to harvest credentials, and pivot into executive accounts; some victims swatted to increase pressure.
incidents.impact: Data theft from SaaS environments (Microsoft Graph, Salesforce, SharePoint) and seven-figure ransom demands; campaign ongoing since February against multiple unnamed victims, per Unit 42 and RH-ISAC.

incidents.vector: Phishing campaign delivered via the Signal messaging app targeting top German decision makers.
incidents.impact: Data leak from compromised officials confirmed; campaign halted and affected individuals notified, but Berlin cannot rule out additional victims.

incidents.vector: Data stolen earlier in 2026 by ShinyHunters in a broader campaign that also hit Zara, 7-Eleven, Udemy and Medtronic.
incidents.impact: Personal data of 7.5 million Carnival customers leaked online after extortion attempts.

incidents.victim: US federal agencies and other Cisco firewall operators
incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in Cisco ASA / Firepower Threat Defense firmware to deploy FIRESTARTER, a persistence mechanism that survives firmware patching.
incidents.impact: Persistent remote access to compromised firewalls remained even after patching; CISA updated Emergency Directive 25-03 and issued a malware analysis report co-sealed with NCSC-UK and ACSC. All FCEB agencies must hunt for FIRESTARTER and apply new vendor updates.

incidents.vector: Hackers breached the French agency that manages identity documents, driving licenses and vehicle registrations.
incidents.impact: Stolen identity-document data being sold by criminals; French government has confirmed the breach and is investigating scope.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and allied militaries (Greece, Romania, Serbia)
incidents.vector: Targeted email compromise of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors plus military personnel in Greece, Romania and Serbia.
incidents.impact: Investigative material accessed; espionage against Ukrainian war-crimes prosecutions and allied defense organizations.

incidents.vector: Breach of OneIndia's digital streaming platforms by group calling itself "True Muslim Afghans."
incidents.impact: Political message attacking India's relationship with the Taliban broadcast across OneIndia's services to 30M+ subscribers.

incidents.vector: Infostealer infection of a Context.ai employee allowed attackers (ShinyHunters) to pivot from the AI vendor's Google Workspace into Vercel's cloud infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Vercel customer credentials and cloud infrastructure data stolen and now being auctioned online; fallout has expanded to additional customers and third-party systems.

incidents.vector: Data stolen from Hallmark's Salesforce instance during the broader 2025 ShinyHunters campaign; published after Hallmark refused to pay a ransom.
incidents.impact: Personal information of 1.7 million Hallmark customers leaked online.

incidents.vector: Targeted OT/ICS malware ("ZionSiphon") designed to identify Israeli water and desalination operators, then drive pump pressure and chlorine to maximum settings; deployment failed due to a buggy IP-range check.
incidents.impact: Sabotage attempt blocked by the malware's own bug; sample identified by Darktrace, contains political messages supporting Iran, Palestine and Yemen.

incidents.vector: Hot wallets compromised at the US-sanctioned exchange; Grinex publicly attributes the breach to "Western intelligence agencies."
incidents.impact: $13M (about 1B rubles) drained; Grinex shut down operations following the theft. The exchange was previously sanctioned for laundering ransomware payments and helping Russia evade sanctions.

incidents.vector: Pro-Russian hacktivist intrusion against a thermal power plant in western Sweden last spring, newly disclosed.
incidents.impact: Disruption attempt blocked by the plant's built-in safeguards; Swedish authorities have linked the group to Russia's security services.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack of unknown nature on the city's IT network on Friday; Leon County severed its connection to prevent contamination — language consistent with a ransomware incident.
incidents.impact: City IT network shut down; multiple city websites and public services remained unavailable into the following week.

incidents.vector: March cyberattack on Hasbro's internal corporate systems.
incidents.impact: Q1 financial results delayed; shipping, invoicing and financial close processes disrupted while systems are restored.

incidents.vector: Breach of Itron's internal corporate systems discovered April 13.
incidents.impact: Incident disclosed and reported as resolved; cyber insurance expected to cover most costs. No customer impact disclosed.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of a vulnerability in the Kelp DAO smart contracts to withdraw funds as restaked-Ether (rsETH) tokens.
incidents.impact: $292M stolen — the largest crypto heist of 2026 to date; the breach triggered a $10B run on Aave, the largest DeFi lending protocol, prompting an industry-wide rescue effort.

incidents.victim: LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Trivy and Telnyx (PyPI) plus downstream consumers
incidents.vector: TeamPCP used likely-stolen maintainer credentials to inject credential-harvesting payloads into trusted packages and poison Checkmarx GitHub Actions workflows; an earlier Trivy compromise supplied additional access.
incidents.impact: AI API keys, cloud credentials and secrets harvested at scale across five software ecosystems in five days; Vect 2.0 ransomware now being deployed against affected organizations. Recorded Future links the campaign to broader credential-driven fraud (payroll redirection, double-brokering of freight loads).

incidents.vector: Security breach exposed customer data; root cause not publicly disclosed.
incidents.impact: Customer names, emails, home addresses, phone numbers and booking details exposed; Booking.com reset reservation PINs. No financial data exposed per the company.
